title: Port of Jiangyin
summary: The Port of Jiangyin (simplified Chinese: 江阴港; traditional Chinese: 江陰港; pinyin: Jiāngyīn Gǎng) is a natural inland port located at Jiangyin, Jiangsu, People's Republic of China. It extends over 35 kilometres (22 mi) of the southern shore of the Yangtze river.  The port had a container throughput of 1,001,000 TEU in 2013.
text:
The Port of Jiangyin (simplified Chinese: 江阴港; traditional Chinese: 江陰港; pinyin: Jiāngyīn Gǎng) is a natural inland port located at Jiangyin, Jiangsu, People's Republic of China. It extends over 35 kilometres (22 mi) of the southern shore of the Yangtze river.  The port had a container throughput of 1,001,000 TEU in 2013.

Setting
The Port of Jiangyin is located in the plain between the Yangtze River and the Lake Tai.

Layout
The Port has 7 deep-water berths, capable of handling ships of up to 100,000 DWT. The channel depth is 15 metres (49 ft) and the land area is of 104 hectares (260 acres).
